headline: Futures rebound Mon pre-open: Nasdaq +0.68%, S&P +0.29%, Dow +0.16% — chips lead bounce after -10% SOX week
summary: US futures higher into Mon session as semis attempt a snap-back after the worst PHLX Semi (SOX) weekly loss since Apr 2025 (-10%). S&P -1.55% / Nasdaq -2.9% last week on TSMC 2nm dilution + SK Hynix/Samsung rout. Investors positioning ahead of TSLA/GOOG (Wed AH), IBM/GEV (Wed pre), INTC (Thu AH). Iran strikes + Brent ~$88 the cross-current.

headline: Hut 8 signs SECOND 352 MW / $9.8B Beacon Point lease — 1 GW Texas campus fully commercialized; $19.6B base-term contract value, $26.6B portfolio
summary: Second 15-year AI datacenter lease at Beacon Point (Nueces County, TX) doubles the same investment-grade tenant's contracted capacity to 704 MW. Portfolio-wide contracted IT capacity now 949 MW / $26.6B base-term value / >$1.75B average annual NOI at stabilization; renewal options push potential to $50.2B. First 352 MW lease was signed Jul 14. Not a tracked ticker but headline validation for the AI-power-first datacenter thesis (read-across VRT, ETN, GEV, VST, CEG). Benchmark PT $165.

headline: Alibaba previews Qwen 3.8 Max at WAIC Shanghai — 2.4T-param sparse MoE multimodal, claims 'second only to Fable 5'
summary: Qwen 3.8-Max-Preview (Sat/Sun window at World AI Conference Shanghai) is Alibaba's first multimodal model above 1T params (text/image/video/documents in). Follows Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 (2.8T open-weight; Alibaba owns 36%). Ranking is internal — no benchmark table, no HuggingFace repo, weights promised 'soon'. Read-across: China-frontier AI resurgence reignites the Nvidia/AMD China-share and inference-elsewhere debate; on the flip side, more parameter count = more compute demand irrespective of geography.

headline: US-Iran strikes into third weekend: US hits Qeshm Island + southern Iran; Iran targets Kuwait power/water/oil for 3rd day; Brent ~$88, WTI ~$82
summary: Nine-day-old ceasefire collapse continues. Kuwaiti authorities report damage at an oil facility; several workers injured. Fresh US strikes triggered by earlier Iranian attack in Jordan that killed two US service members. Gasoline > $4/gal. Strait of Hormuz shipping remains at a fraction of pre-war throughput; power/desal infrastructure attacks widen the target set. Watch energy names + risk-off tape into the open.

headline: CLSA initiates MSFT Outperform PT $535 — Azure ~40% growth, 20M Copilot vs 450M Office paid seats = long runway
summary: Fresh Outperform rating with $535 PT built on 25x Dec-2027 normalized EPS (1 SD below 5-year avg — argues valuation already de-risked). Bull thesis: Azure ~40% growth, rest of business ~10%; Copilot penetration 20M / 450M paid Office seats = 4% and rising. Vote of confidence for the tracked STRONG_BUY name into the Jul 30 earnings print.

headline: CLSA initiates ORCL Hold PT $145 on AI-cloud debt-funding math: '$500B capex to 2030 vs internal cash only ~1/5'
summary: CLSA argues Oracle needs up to $500B in capital through 2030 to hit management's AI cloud goals, but internal cash generation supports only ~20% — making ORCL a high-beta debt-funded story. $145 PT = 18x FY28 GAAP EPS (20% premium to CLSA's SaaS peer multiple). Stock -33% MoM already; this is a cautious call ratifying (rather than driving) the derating. Tracked HOLD; watch for capex commentary at earnings.

headline: Barclays upgrades Lumentum (LITE) to Overweight, PT $1,000 — optical/photonic AI-networking read-across for ANET, GLW
summary: Ahead of the Aug semi-earnings block, Barclays takes LITE to OW from EW at $1,000 PT. Signal: sell-side conviction on AI datacenter optics/transceivers holding through the SOX drawdown. LITE not tracked, but ANET (switching), GLW (fiber) benefit from the same 400G/800G/1.6T buildout that drives Lumentum's InP/EML/CW-laser mix. Small confirming datapoint alongside AMAT's Jul 13 GS raise to $645.

headline: GE Vernova Q2 preview Wed Jul 22 pre-open: cons. EPS $3.17 / rev $10.77B (+18.2% YoY); gas turbine + grid drive, wind still $200-300M EBITDA drag
summary: Q2 report Wed 7:30 AM ET call. AI-power demand thesis expected to lift Power (gas turbines) + Electrification (grid) segments; Wind revenue guided to decline mid-teens YoY with $200-300M EBITDA loss. Key focus items: gas turbine backlog (was $126B+ at Q1), $18B pipeline for onshoring, updated FY guide. Read-across for VRT (Aug 6), ETN (Aug 5), power complex.

headline: Siemens Energy to rebrand as 'Omterra' — announced Jul 14; 1-2 year transition, cuts ~EUR300M/yr Siemens brand licensing payments
summary: Multi-year phased switch (starting 2026). Name = 'Omega' (electrical resistance) + 'Terra' (earth). Removes recurring ~EUR300M/yr royalty to Siemens AG — small margin tailwind. Stock ENR.DE EUR 146.90 Thu close = -24% vs Apr 24 high (EUR 195.54) on AI-capex-slowdown chatter + ABB deal read-through. Barclays trimmed to Underweight PT EUR 130 Jul 15. Reminder for the tracked name into 8 Aug F3Q print.
